data.title: Stephen Yiu’s Blue Whale dumps Microsoft, Meta; adds Nvidia
data.description:
A Blue Whale Capital LLP investment fund, supported by UK billionaire Peter Hargreaves, has completely divested stakes in Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) after lowering stakes in both the technology firms last year, the Financial Times reported Friday, citing the investment fund’s manager.
 
 
Fund manager Stephen Yiu commented that tariffs imposed by the U.S. could lead to global economic slowdown, eventually impacting Meta's digital advertising business. Meta constituted about 3.3% of this portfolio.
 
 
The investment firm also exited Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) as ramp-up in artificial intelligence investments could soon outweigh cash generation. The fund’s Microsoft holding was 2.11% at the end of 2024 down from 8% in January 2024.
 
 
On the other hand, Yiu has increased the fund’s holdings in Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) as the stock price is down almost 24% from the start of the year. The fund held 1.09M shares of the tech firm at a notional market value of $146.86M at the end of 4Q24.
 
 
The Blue Whale Capital LLP investment fund manages assets worth $1.34 billion as on 31st Dec, 2024, with technology stocks contributing $788.7M.

data.title: Is the stock market open on Good Friday?
data.description:
Traders will get some time off from the stock market in observance of Good Friday, and equity index futures won't be active. Many global markets are also closed, with exceptions including Japan and mainland China.

The U.S. bond market as well as the metals and commodities market - including for Nymex crude oil and Comex-traded gold - will be closed too. Cryptocurrency markets remain open as usual, with bitcoin (BTC-USD) marginally higher at $84,602 at the time of writing.

No economic data is scheduled for Friday. San Francisco Federal Reserve President Mary Daly is due to speak later in the day, and may offer clues on the economy and future monetary policy. 

See which companies are scheduled to report earnings on Friday.

Some history: While Good Friday is not an official federal U.S. holiday, Wall Street has historically observed this holiday since the late 19th century. There were only three exceptions when the New York Stock Exchange remained open on Good Friday - 1898, 1906 and 1907.

data.title: Arctic Star CFO steps down
data.description:
• Arctic Star Exploration (TSXV:ADD:CA) said that Binny Jassal has resigned as the company’s CFO.
  • Following Mr. Jassal’s resignation, James Ferreira has agreed to resume the role of interim CFO while the company searches for a suitable replacement.

data.title: U.S. unveils port fees for Chinese ships after industry pushback
data.description:
The Trump administration has scaled back its plan to charge port fees on Chinese ships docking at U.S. ports, with the aim of limiting disruption for domestic exporters and shipowners.

The new "targeted action" will begin to "reverse Chinese dominance, address threats to the U.S. supply chain, and send a demand signal for U.S.-built ships," said U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

Fees on Chinese ship operators and owners will be based on net tonnage of the vessel. If a ship makes multiple U.S. entries before transiting to a foreign destination, this fee is assessed per rotation or string of U.S. port calls.

"The fee will be set at $0 for the first 180 days (till October 14), will then be set at $50/net tonnage, and will increase incrementally over the next three years," according to the USTR's notice.

Fees on Chinese-built ships will start at $18 a ton from October 14, or $120 for each container, and will increase over the next three years. The fees will be charged up to five times a year, per vessel.

The USTR will not charge any fee based on fleet composition for now. Ships exempt from the fees: ships enrolled in certain U.S. Maritime Administration programs, vessels arriving empty or in ballast, smaller ships, U.S.-owned companies' vessels, and specialized export vessels.

The new actions addressed concerns raised by the global maritime industry, which opposed the USTR's earlier proposal of a flat fee of up to $1.5M for each Chinese-built vessel entering U.S. ports, and escalating the fees based on the percentage of Chinese-built ships in the operator's fleet.

Shipping stocks: Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY), Frontline (NYSE:FRO), Dorian LPG (NYSE:LPG), Navigator (NYSE:NVGS), International Seaways (NYSE:INSW), Teekay Tankers (NYSE:TNK), DHT (NYSE:DHT), Hafnia (NYSE:HAFN), Scorpio Tankers (NYSE:STNG), Torm (NASDAQ:TRMD), Ardmore Shipping (NYSE:ASC), Safe Bulkers (NYSE:SB), Star Bulk Carriers (NASDAQ:SBLK), Genco Shipping (NYSE:GNK), Tsakos Energy (NYSE:TEN), ZIM (NYSE:ZIM), Costamare (NYSE:CMRE), Danaos (NYSE:DAC), Matson (NYSE:MATX), COSCO (OTCPK:CICOF).

data.title: Psyence BioMed gets Nasdaq notice for non-compliance

data.description:

  • Revenue of $43.88M (+7.8% Y/Y).

data.title: European markets closed for Easter holiday
data.description:
London (UKX) closed. On Thursday, the index was almost flat.

Germany (DAX:IND) closed. Dropped marginally 0.50% the previous day.

France (CAC:IND) closed. Dropped 0.60% from the previous day.

Slovakia posted a current account deficit of EUR 667.4 million in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) closed. Dropped 0.63% on the previous day.

The bond markets also remained closed; the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield gained 5 basis points the previous day to 4.33%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.48%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.57%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Lake Winn announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Lake Winn Resources (TSXV:LWR:CA) said that Binny Jassal resigned as the company’s CFO and as a director of the company.
  • Following Mr. Jassal’s resignation, James Ferreira has agreed to resume the role of interim CFO while the company searches for a suitable replacement.

data.title: Ukraine and U.S. ink memorandum of intent, paving way for critical minerals deal
data.description:
Ukraine has signed a memorandum of intent with the U.S., signaling progress towards a critical minerals deal that had been temporarily derailed following a heated meeting between presidents Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy in February.

"We are happy to announce the signing, with our American partners, of a Memorandum of Intent, which paves the way for an Economic Partnership Agreement and the establishment of the Investment Fund for the Reconstruction of Ukraine," said Yulia Svyrydenko, Ukraine's economy minister.

"It is very important that the document notes the desire of the American people to invest together with the Ukrainian people in a free, sovereign, and secure Ukraine," she added.

Details of the pact were not disclosed. The text of the agreement needs to be finalized, signed, and then ratified by the countries' parliaments.

"There is a lot to do, but the current pace and significant progress give reason to expect that the document will be very beneficial for both countries," Svyrydenko noted. 

At a press event on Thursday alongside Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, Trump said a deal would be signed soon. "We have a minerals deal, which I guess is going to be signed next Thursday (April 24)? Soon. I assume they're (Ukraine) going to live up to the deal."

"We're still working on the details," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent clarified, adding that the deal is substantially what was agreed upon previously. "We're shooting for around April 26."
